---
title: "Quarto JS Demo"
author: # more info: https://quarto.org/docs/journals/authors.html#author-metadata
  - name: Bill Gates
    orcid: 0000-0003-1689-0557
    email: bill@gates.com
    affiliations:
      - name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation
        address: 440 5th Ave N
        city: Seattle
        state: WA
        postal-code: 98109-4631
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: "images/cat.jpg"
  data-background-size: cover
  data-background-position: center
  data-background-opacity: "0.5"
---

# Quarto Slides Basics - Write your slides in markdown!


## Start With a Markdown File

Start your file with a block of YAML frontmatter:

```yaml
---
title: This Presentation's Title
---
```


---

## Add Some Sections

Heading level 1 makes new sections, which will start with their own title slide.

```markdown
# This Will Make a New Section
```

---

## Then Add Some Slides

Add Titles with Heading Level 2:

```markdown
## My First Slide

The content of the slide
```

A horizontal rule will separate slides

```markdown
## Slide 1

Content

---

## Slide 2

More Content
```

# Making Slides With Code

## Presenting Code

Add a fenced code block with a language declaration, and line highlights in a curly field after:

````quarto
```{.python code-line-numbers="1|2|3-5"}
@app.route('/members/view/<int:id>')
def view_member(id): # <1>
    member = Members.query.get_or_404(id)
    memberships = Memberships.query.all()
    return render_template('view_member.html', member=member, memberships=memberships) # <2>

```
````

---

## Result:

No lines highlighted!

```{.python }
@app.route('/members/view/<int:id>')
def view_member(id): # <1>
    member = Members.query.get_or_404(id)
    memberships = Memberships.query.all()
    return render_template('view_member.html', member=member, memberships=memberships) # <2>
```

---

## Result:

Highlight empty. Big code block!

```{.python code-line-numbers="" startFrom='100'}
@app.route('/members/view/<int:id>')
def view_member(id): # <1>
    member = Members.query.get_or_404(id)
    memberships = Memberships.query.all()
    return render_template('view_member.html', member=member, memberships=memberships) # <2>
@app.route('/members/view/<int:id>')
def view_member(id): # <1>
    member = Members.query.get_or_404(id)
    memberships = Memberships.query.all()
    return render_template('view_member.html', member=member, memberships=memberships) # <2>
@app.route('/members/view/<int:id>')
def view_member(id): # <1>
    member = Members.query.get_or_404(id)
    memberships = Memberships.query.all()
    return render_template('view_member.html', member=member, memberships=memberships) # <2>
@app.route('/members/view/<int:id>')
def view_member(id): # <1>
    member = Members.query.get_or_404(id)
    memberships = Memberships.query.all()
    return render_template('view_member.html', member=member, memberships=memberships) # <2>
@app.route('/members/view/<int:id>')
def view_member(id): # <1>
    member = Members.query.get_or_404(id)
    memberships = Memberships.query.all()
    return render_template('view_member.html', member=member, memberships=memberships) # <2>
```

---

## Result:

Full highlight.

```{.python code-line-numbers="1|2|3-5"}
@app.route('/members/view\/<int:id>')
def view_member(id): # <1>
    member = Members.query.get_or_404(id)
    memberships = Memberships.query.all()
    return render_template('view_member.html', member=member, memberships=memberships) # <2>
```

---

## Annotating Code

You can add annotations to code!!

Put numbers in blocks like `# <1>` at the end of lines.

This should be whatever the language's comment notation is, then angle brackets around a number.

Then put an ordered list right after the code block!

```markdown
1. First Thing
2. Second Thing
```

---

## Here Is Some Annotated Python Code

```{.python filename="app.py"}
@app.route('/members/view/<int:id>')
def view_member(id): # <1>
    member = Members.query.get_or_404(id)
    memberships = Memberships.query.all()
    return render_template('view_member.html', member=member, memberships=memberships) # <2>
```
1. Start with the view_member function
2. Make sure the return statement returns a view

---

## Some annotated CSS

```{.css code-line-numbers="true" code-copy=true code-annotations=bottom code-block-height=100px}
.car,
.enemy {
position: absolute;
bottom: 100px;
margin: auto;
width: 50px;
height: 100px;
background-color: white;    /* <1> */
line-height: 38px;
font-size: 1.7em;
text-align: center;
vertical-align: middle;
background-image: url(car2.png);
background-size: cover;
}
.line {
position: absolute;         /* <2> */
height: 100px;              /* <2> */
width: 10px;                /* <2> */
margin-left: 195px;         /* <2> */
background-color: white;    /* <2> */
}
.score {
background-color: black;    /* <3> */
height: 70px;               /* <3> */
text-align: center;         /* <3> */
color: white;               /* <3> */
font-size: 1.5em;           /* <3> */
font-family: fantasy;       /* <3> */
}
.gameArea {
background-color: black;    /* <4> */
width: 400px;               /* <4> */
height: 100%;               /* <4> */
overflow: hidden;           /* <4> */
position: relative;         /* <4> */
margin: auto;               /* <4> */
}

.startScreen {
position: absolute;
background-color: red;      /* <5> */
left: 25%;
top: 100px;
color: white;               /* <6> */
z-index: 1;
text-align: center;
border: 1px solid red;
padding: 15px;
margin: auto;
width: 50%;
font-family: sans-serif;
}
```
1. Change color of your car (enemy cars will get random colors in javascript)
2. Change anything about the road lines
3. Changes colors or sizing of score board
4. Change the color of the road
5. Change background color of start screen
6. Change text color of start screen

---

## Some JS

```js
console.log("First!!!!") // <1>
console.log("Second!!!!!!") // <2>
```
1. First
2. Second

---

## Code Block Sizes

This theme includes some logic for custom code block sizes.

The sizes are defined in the `dunwoody.scss` theme in the `/*-- scss:defaults --*/` section at the top. Adding another entry should work without trouble if you follow the format of the others. These sizes use `vh` units as a percentage of viewport size, so they should scale with the presentation size. These are also maximum heights, so shorter code will still shrink to the smallest height possible.

Available sizes:

- Tall Code (`.tall-code`) - 85vh
- Medium Code (`.med-code`) - 60vh
- Short Code (`.short-code`) - 35vh
- Micro Code (`.micro-code`) - 20vh

You can apply these to any code block by adding the appropriate class to the code block:

````quarto
```{.css .tall-code}
# code here
```
````

---

## Tall code!


```{.css .tall-code}
.car,
.enemy {
position: absolute;
bottom: 100px;
margin: auto;
width: 50px;
height: 100px;
background-color: white;    /* <1> */
line-height: 38px;
font-size: 1.7em;
text-align: center;
vertical-align: middle;
background-image: url(car2.png);
background-size: cover;
}
.line {
position: absolute;         /* <2> */
height: 100px;              /* <2> */
width: 10px;                /* <2> */
margin-left: 195px;         /* <2> */
background-color: white;    /* <2> */
}
.score {
background-color: black;    /* <3> */
height: 70px;               /* <3> */
text-align: center;         /* <3> */
color: white;               /* <3> */
font-size: 1.5em;           /* <3> */
font-family: fantasy;       /* <3> */
}
```

---

## Medium code!


```{.css .med-code}
.car,
.enemy {
position: absolute;
bottom: 100px;
margin: auto;
width: 50px;
height: 100px;
background-color: white;    /* <1> */
line-height: 38px;
font-size: 1.7em;
text-align: center;
vertical-align: middle;
background-image: url(car2.png);
background-size: cover;
}
.line {
position: absolute;         /* <2> */
height: 100px;              /* <2> */
width: 10px;                /* <2> */
margin-left: 195px;         /* <2> */
background-color: white;    /* <2> */
}
.score {
background-color: black;    /* <3> */
height: 70px;               /* <3> */
text-align: center;         /* <3> */
color: white;               /* <3> */
font-size: 1.5em;           /* <3> */
font-family: fantasy;       /* <3> */
}
```

---

## Short code!


```{.css .short-code}
.car,
.enemy {
position: absolute;
bottom: 100px;
margin: auto;
width: 50px;
height: 100px;
background-color: white;    /* <1> */
line-height: 38px;
font-size: 1.7em;
text-align: center;
vertical-align: middle;
background-image: url(car2.png);
background-size: cover;
}
.line {
position: absolute;         /* <2> */
height: 100px;              /* <2> */
width: 10px;                /* <2> */
margin-left: 195px;         /* <2> */
background-color: white;    /* <2> */
}
.score {
background-color: black;    /* <3> */
height: 70px;               /* <3> */
text-align: center;         /* <3> */
color: white;               /* <3> */
font-size: 1.5em;           /* <3> */
font-family: fantasy;       /* <3> */
}
```
---

## Micro code!


```{.css .micro-code}
.car,
.enemy {
position: absolute;
bottom: 100px;
margin: auto;
width: 50px;
height: 100px;
background-color: white;    /* <1> */
line-height: 38px;
font-size: 1.7em;
text-align: center;
vertical-align: middle;
background-image: url(car2.png);
background-size: cover;
}
.line {
position: absolute;         /* <2> */
height: 100px;              /* <2> */
width: 10px;                /* <2> */
margin-left: 195px;         /* <2> */
background-color: white;    /* <2> */
}
.score {
background-color: black;    /* <3> */
height: 70px;               /* <3> */
text-align: center;         /* <3> */
color: white;               /* <3> */
font-size: 1.5em;           /* <3> */
font-family: fantasy;       /* <3> */
}
```

# Some More Tricks

## Columns

Add columns by using div markers:
```markdown
::: {.columns}


::: {.column width="70%"}
Stuff in first columns
:::

::: {.column width="70%"}
Stuff in second column
:::

:::
```

---

## Columns Example

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="70%"}
Here is some very simple html code:

```html
<h1>My Page</h1>
<p>Here is a paragraph.</p>
<img width="200px" src="./images/cat.jpg"/>
```
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
And the result:

<div style="border: 2px white dashed">

<h1>My Page</h1>
<p>Here is a paragraph.</p>
<img width="200px" src="./images/cat.jpg"/>

</div>
:::

:::

---

## Make Big Text


A div with `r-fit-text`:
```markdown
::: r-fit-text
This will be as big as possible!
:::
```

Will look like this:


::: r-fit-text
This is big!
:::

---


## Images

Include images by linking to them markdown style:

```markdown
![](./images/cat.jpg){.absolute bottom=0 height="60%"}
```

![](./images/cat.jpg){.absolute bottom=0 height="60%"}

---

## Positioning Images


(under construction)!!!!!

![Look at how nicely this image works on the side over there, even when this text starts to get really long.]{.absolute style:"max-width=20%"}

![](./images/cat.jpg){.absolute top="-10%" right="-10%" height="120%" style="max-height: unset;"}

---

## Incremental Lists

You can make lists incremental, meaning they will display items one-by-one as you progress the presentation.

You can make everything incremental by editing the yaml frontmatter:

```yaml
title: "My Presentation"
format:
  revealjs:
    incremental: true
```

Or make one specific thing incremental by surrounding it with a div with an explicit class:
````quarto
::: {.incremental}
- First
- Second
- Third
:::
````

::: {.incremental}
- First
- Second
- Third
:::

---

## Formatting Lists

You can find more info on formatting lists here: [Quarto Guide](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/markdown-basics.html#lists)
